description:
Recto: Letter or petition in Judaeo-Arabic. Goitein suggests that it was written to the judge Avraham b. Natan, who reused it for the account on verso. The sender is a woman. Only the first part of each line is preserved, so it is difficult to understand the content. It seems that her husband has been traveling for over a year while she remains at home. It seems that some people wanted to manumit the daughter of a female slave (fa-rāmū ʿitq ibnat al-jā[riya]). Only fragmentary phrases are preserved out of the next section: the number 10; someone testifying "about her"; "her document"; the court; "the remainder of the ketubba"; "she entered the house"; and "the first."

The margin contains an extended dialogue between the speaker and a man, presumably her husband: "I said to him, 'You returned(?) [...], and the sign is our reconciliation and my demanding the ketubba from you,' and he said, 'I know that, but we will take you(?),' and I said to him by way of rejecting him/this, 'I have no replacement.' He said, 'If . . . from your dower (mahr). . . .'
description_he: רקטו: מכתב או בקשה ביהודית-ערבית. גויטין מציע שנכתב לשופט אברהם בן נתן, שהשתמש בו מחדש לחשבון שבוורסו. השולחת היא אישה. רק החלק הראשון של כל שורה שמור, ולכן קשה להבין את התוכן. נראה שבעלה נסע ליותר משנה בעוד היא נשארת בבית. השוליים מכילים שיחה מורחבת בין הדוברת ואיש, כנראה בעלה.
